content.text: Grounding Data Quality Metrics and Thresholds

The quality of a Gen AI application's responses is bounded by the quality of the data it retrieves. A model cannot compensate for an incomplete, stale, or mislabelled knowledge base — it will either hallucinate to fill gaps, serve outdated information confidently, or retrieve documents that violate access control because their sensitivity labels are wrong. Grounding data quality metrics quantify the health of the retrieval layer across five dimensions: completeness, freshness, label accuracy, retrieval coverage, and consistency. Threshold gates convert those metrics into actionable traffic-light signals that stewards and platform engineers can act on.

This topic defines each metric dimension, provides Python implementations for computing them from the chunk provenance registry and retrieval log, sets recommended thresholds, and explains how the composite quality score feeds the governance dashboard.

attrs.alt: Grounding data quality metrics dashboard and threshold gates

content.text: Every chunk has doc_id, sensitivity_label, source_uri, indexed_utc, and embedding_model
Chunks missing metadata bypass label gating or break provenance queries
Each document was ingested within its steward-committed max_age_days window
Stale documents produce outdated answers that can mislead or cause compliance failures
Spot-checked documents carry the label the domain steward would assign
Under-labelled docs leak RESTRICTED content to lower-clearance users; over-labelled docs unnecessarily block access
The knowledge base can answer the range of topics the application is designed for
Coverage gaps cause the model to hallucinate rather than cite a source
No two chunks in the collection contain substantially the same text (Jaccard similarity < 0.85)
Near-duplicate chunks waste vector store space and inflate retrieval scores for one document

Label accuracy cannot be computed fully automatically because it requires a human steward to validate the label against the actual document content. The measurement process draws a stratified random sample (weighted toward higher sensitivity tiers because errors there have greater impact) and presents each sampled document to the domain steward for verification. The accuracy score is the fraction of sampled documents that the steward confirms as correctly labelled.

Retrieval coverage measures whether the knowledge base contains documents relevant to the query categories the application is designed to handle. It is measured with a curated test query set: a list of representative questions across the application's intended topic space. For each test query, the retriever runs against the live knowledge base; a query has coverage if it returns at least one chunk above the minimum similarity threshold.

from __future__ import annotations
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional

@dataclass
class CoverageQueryResult:
    query_text:     str
    topic_category: str
    has_coverage:   bool    # True if at least one chunk retrieved above threshold
    top_score:      float   # best retrieval similarity score (0 if no results)
    top_chunk_id:   Optional[str] = None

@dataclass
class RetrievalCoverageResult:
    total_queries:      int
    covered_queries:    int
    uncovered:          list[CoverageQueryResult]   # queries with no results
    low_score:          list[CoverageQueryResult]   # results but score < 0.6
    coverage_pct:       float

MIN_COVERAGE_SCORE = 0.60

def compute_retrieval_coverage(
    test_queries: list[dict],     # [{"query": str, "category": str}]
    embed_fn,                     # callable(str) -> list[float]
    retriever,                    # ProvenanceAwareRetriever
    collection: str,
    user_label: str = "INTERNAL",
    lineage_id: str = "coverage_test",
) -> RetrievalCoverageResult:
    results: list[CoverageQueryResult] = []
    for q in test_queries:
        embedding = embed_fn(q["query"])
        chunks = retriever.retrieve(embedding, collection, top_k=3,
                                    user_label=user_label, lineage_id=lineage_id)
        top_score = chunks[0].score if chunks else 0.0
        results.append(CoverageQueryResult(
            query_text=q["query"],
            topic_category=q["category"],
            has_coverage=top_score >= MIN_COVERAGE_SCORE,
            top_score=top_score,
            top_chunk_id=chunks[0].chunk_id if chunks else None,
        ))

    covered = [r for r in results if r.has_coverage]
    uncovered = [r for r in results if not r.has_coverage]
    low_score  = [r for r in results if r.has_coverage and r.top_score < 0.75]
    return RetrievalCoverageResult(
        total_queries=len(results),
        covered_queries=len(covered),
        uncovered=uncovered,
        low_score=low_score,
        coverage_pct=round(len(covered) / len(results) * 100, 2) if results else 0.0,
    )
Composite Quality Score and Threshold Gates
Threshold Calibration by Application Risk Level

Not all Gen AI applications carry equal risk. A customer-facing support assistant that references regulatory documents requires stricter thresholds than an internal idea-generation tool. The threshold configuration is stored per knowledge base rather than as a global constant.
Legal advice, medical Q&A, regulatory compliance
Internal employee assistant with HR/policy data
A quality metric crossing a threshold is only useful if someone acts on it. The alerting model ties each metric dimension to a specific remediation owner and a maximum response time:
Fix ingestion pipeline to populate missing fields; re-index affected chunks
Trigger overdue review queue; auto-block stale docs; notify domain steward
Correct labelling errors found in sample; re-classify affected documents
Identify topic gaps; source new documents; ingest via normal approval path
Run near-duplicate detection; retain canonical chunk; deindex duplicates

Operational note: completeness and consistency RED statuses are engineering bugs — they indicate that the ingestion pipeline failed, not that the content itself is wrong. They should be treated as production incidents and resolved within hours, not days.

Grounding data quality metrics give the governance programme a quantitative view of knowledge base health across five dimensions that directly affect model output quality and compliance. Completeness and consistency are engineering-controlled metrics computed automatically from the chunk registry. Freshness is controlled by the steward review cadence. Label accuracy requires a human steward to validate a stratified random sample. Retrieval coverage requires a curated test query set and measures whether the knowledge base actually covers the application's intended topic space. The composite quality score and threshold gates produce a traffic-light signal per dimension and an overall status per knowledge base. Ingestion blocking on critical metric failures prevents new low-quality content from compounding existing problems.
